page: Microsoft Azure FX96-48mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor. The system is equipped with 1832.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 38.17 GB per vCPU.

For local storage, the instance includes a 5669 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. No dedicated graphics processing units are included in this configuration, and it does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The hardware profile offers high resource density, particularly regarding memory capacity relative to compute cores. This memory-heavy configuration is designed for workloads requiring massive in-memory data processing without the need for GPU acceleration. Suitable workloads include large-scale in-memory databases, heavy enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data analytics.
description: Microsoft Azure FX96-48mds_v2 is a memory-intensive server instance from the FXmdsv2 family, built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor, paired with 1832.0 GB of system memory, which establishes a high memory-to-core ratio of 38.17 GB per vCPU. Storage capabilities include a 5669 GB local disk with Premium Storage compatibility. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This configuration provides a high-density memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that demand substantial memory capacity without proportional CPU scaling. The server is designed for memory-intensive applications, including large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data analytics pipelines.
